label: Great Lakes Plastic Pollution: Scale and Impact

comments.text: FYI more than 22 million pounds of plastic end up in the #GreatLakes every year. This litter breaks down into microplastics and you humans are drinking it in water (and beer). You’re eating it in fish. 🌊🌊

We eat one credit card every week because of microplastics. These tiny particles have been found in our Great Lakes, our blood, and our organs, yet we don’t know how they impact us. I introduced the Plastic Health Research Act so we can understand the harm and fight back for a healthier future.

About 22 million pounds of plastic end up in Great Lakes every year. Plastic is in all my fish, mammals and humans.
And the beer you humans drink! Please do something.
End plastic pollution NOW 🌊🌊

As experts failed to negotiate a global plastic pollution treaty in Geneva this month, Great Lakes scientists continued their research that could pinpoint how microplastics impact human health and ultimately inform decisions made across the world.

Calling Lake Superior ‘zero pollution’ is disingenuous. Microplastics in every fish, PFAS in the rain, runoff in the water. Cleanest lake? Maybe. Pollution-free? Never.

By one estimate, at least 22 million pounds of plastic waste end up in the Great Lakes every year. Lake Michigan is rivaled only by Lake Erie in concentrations of nurdles and other tiny bits known collectively as microplastics.
